   - Footer separator missing `aria-hidden="true"` → added
   - Buttons missing `type="button"` → added
   - Ran `npx html-validate` - passed clean

9. **Commits made throughout:**

10. **User asks about lightweight local serving** → Discussed `npx serve` and `python3 -m http.server`, chose `npx serve`

11. **User asks to serve on 8788 instead of 8787/landing** → Explored Cloudflare Workers static assets but realized `/` route conflict (serves both image and HTML). Decided to keep separate: landing on :8788, API on :8787

12. **User asks to automate local vs production URL detection** → Changed to `location.hostname === 'localhost' ? 'http://localhost:8787' : PROD_BASE` with URL rewriting for all links, copy buttons, and display text

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wanted to make the landing page (`landing/index.html`) for nasapicture.com use live data from the API server instead of hardcoded APOD data. This expanded into: improving semantic HTML/accessibility, setting up local dev tooling (dual server setup with npm scripts), QA testing the cache system, deploying both the API Worker and landing page to Cloudflare, adding `/image.jpg` and `/image-sd.jpg` extension alias routes, and merging everything to main.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Cloudflare Workers serving NASA APOD proxy API on `api.nasapicture.com`
   - Cloudflare Pages hosting static landing page on `nasapicture.com`
   - Workers Cache API (`caches.default`) for caching APOD metadata and images
   - Auto-detecting localhost vs production for API base URL
   - URL rewriting in JS to swap production URLs for local dev URLs
   - Semantic HTML: `<figure>`/`<figcaption>`, `<main>`, `<time datetime>`, `<nav>`, `aria-hidden`
   - HTML validation via `npx html-validate`
   - Cloudflare Pages deployment: `--branch main` required for production deployment (vs Preview)
   - `npx serve` for lightweight local static file serving
   - Board system: TASK lifecycle (todo → done → archive)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`landing/index.html`** — The landing page, heavily modified this session
     - Removed hardcoded image src, title, date; now fetched from API `/info` endpoint
     - Added `<main>`, wrapped image in `<figure>`, `<figcaption>` with `<p>` and `<time datetime="">`
     - Usage items changed from `<span>` to `<p>`/`<code>`, buttons got `type="button"`
     - SVGs got `aria-hidden="true"`, footer separator got `aria-hidden="true"`
     - Image src placeholder: `data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7`
     - Header uses `flex: 1` on all three children (logo, h1, nav) for true center alignment
     - Caption has `min-width: 30rem` for tall/narrow images
     - Key JS block:
       ```javascript
       const PROD_BASE = 'https://api.nasapicture.com';
       const API_BASE = location.hostname === 'localhost' ? `http://localhost:8787` : PROD_BASE;
       // Rewrite all production URLs to use API_BASE
       const defined_replace = (str) => str.replace(PROD_BASE, API_BASE);
       document.querySelectorAll(`a[href^="${PROD_BASE}"]`).forEach(a => {
         a.href = defined_replace(a.href);
       });
       document.querySelectorAll('.copy-btn[data-copy]').forEach(btn => {
         btn.dataset.copy = defined_replace(btn.dataset.copy);
       });
       document.querySelectorAll('.usage-url').forEach(el => {
         el.childNodes.forEach(node => {
           if (node.nodeType === Node.TEXT_NODE) {
             node.textContent = defined_replace(node.textContent);
           }
         });
       });
       // Fetch APOD metadata, then set image + caption from live data
       fetch(`${API_BASE}/info`)
         .then(r => r.json())
         .then(data => {
           if (data.title) {
             img.src = data.hdurl || data.url;
             img.alt = data.title;
             document.getElementById('caption-title').textContent = data.title;
             const dateEl = document.getElementById('caption-date');
             dateEl.textContent = data.date;
             dateEl.setAttribute('datetime', data.date);
             document.getElementById('hero-caption').classList.add('visible');
           }
         })
         .catch(() => {});
       ```
     - Source link updated to `https://github.com/GDKeller/nasa-picture-proxy`

   - **`src/index.ts`** — The Cloudflare Worker, added extension alias routes
     - Added `sdPaths = ["/sd", "/image-sd.jpg"]` and `hdPaths = ["/", "/image.jpg"]`
     - Route matching changed from `pathname === "/" || pathname === "/sd"` to `hdPaths.includes(pathname) || sdPaths.includes(pathname)`
     - Updated `/info` endpoints object and `/about` route listing to document new routes

   - **`package.json`** — Added dev tooling scripts
     ```json
     "dev": "mkdir -p logs && wrangler dev > logs/api.log 2>&1 & npx serve landing --listen 8788 > logs/landing.log 2>&1 & echo 'Dev servers started\n  API:     http://localhost:8787\n  Landing: http://localhost:8788\n\nRun npm run log to watch output'",
     "api": "wrangler dev",
     "dev:landing": "npx serve landing --listen 8788",
     "log": "tail -f logs/api.log logs/landing.log",
     "log:tail": "tail -20 logs/api.log logs/landing.log",
     "stop": "lsof -ti:8787,8788 | xargs kill 2>/dev/null && echo 'Dev servers stopped' || echo 'No servers running'",
     ```

   - **`wrangler.toml`** — Briefly had `[assets]` config added then removed (conflict with `/` route). Unchanged from original.

   - **`.gitignore`** — Added `logs/`

   - **`README.md`** — Added `https://github.com/GDKeller/nasa-picture-proxy` link at top

   - **`board/tasks/TASK-0004_qa-cache-logging-deploy.md`** — Created for QA task
   - **`board/archive/TASK-0001_build-landing-page.md`** — Marked done and archived

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Absolute positioning for h1 centering**: User explicitly said "use flexbox to do it, not absolute positioning" → Changed to `flex: 1` on all three header children
   - **`min-width: 480px`**: User said "we should do it in rem so it will adjust with font size" → Changed to `30rem`
   - **`figcaption` outside `figure`**: HTML validator would fail → Wrapped img+figcaption in `<figure>`
   - **Buttons missing `type="button"`**: `npx html-validate` caught this → Added `type="button"` to copy buttons
   - **`file://` protocol detection**: Added then reverted when switching to `npx serve` (localhost detection sufficient)
   - **`pkill -f 'wrangler dev'`**: User said "that's worse, i have other wrangler dev servers" → Reverted to port-based kill
   - **Wrangler not found**: `wrangler dev` failed in background, needed `npx wrangler dev`
   - **Pages deploy to Preview**: First `wrangler pages deploy` didn't specify `--branch main`, so it went to Preview environment instead of Production → Redeployed with `--branch main`
   - **Git checkout with uncommitted changes**: Stashed specstory files before merge, popped after

5. Problem Solving:
   - Solved landing page hardcoded data by fetching from `/info` API endpoint
   - Solved local dev URL issue with `location.hostname === 'localhost'` detection + URL rewriting
   - Solved the "serve landing locally" problem by using `npx serve` on port 8788
   - Explored and rejected Workers static assets approach due to `/` route conflict (image vs HTML)
   - QA'd all 4 routes (`/`, `/sd`, `/info`, `/about`) — cache working correctly (MISS then HIT)
   - Successfully deployed API Worker and landing page to Cloudflare
   - Added `/image.jpg` and `/image-sd.jpg` for contexts needing file extensions

6. All user messages:
   - "I have the api server running on 8787. on the landing page replace the hardcoded data with live data from the api"

   - "if TASK-0001 is to be marked done (which it is), then shouldnt it be archived" → "yes and create the new TASK"

432	  <script>
433	    const PROD_BASE = 'https://api.nasapicture.com';
434	    const API_BASE = location.hostname === 'localhost' ? `http://localhost:8787` : PROD_BASE;
435	    const img = document.getElementById('hero-img');
436	
437	    function onImageReady() { img.classList.add('loaded'); }
438	
439	    // Rewrite all production URLs to use API_BASE
440	    const defined_replace = (str) => str.replace(PROD_BASE, API_BASE);
441	    document.querySelectorAll(`a[href^="${PROD_BASE}"]`).forEach(a => {
442	      a.href = defined_replace(a.href);
443	    });
444	    document.querySelectorAll('.copy-btn[data-copy]').forEach(btn => {
445	      btn.dataset.copy = defined_replace(btn.dataset.copy);
446	    });
447	    document.querySelectorAll('.usage-url').forEach(el => {
448	      el.childNodes.forEach(node => {
449	        if (node.nodeType === Node.TEXT_NODE) {
450	          node.textContent = defined_replace(node.textContent);
451	        }
452	      });
453	    });
454	
455	    // Fetch APOD metadata, then set image + caption from live data
456	    fetch(`${API_BASE}/info`)
457	      .then(r => r.json())
458	      .then(data => {
459	        if (data.title) {
460	          img.src = data.hdurl || data.url;
461	          img.alt = data.title;
462	          document.getElementById('caption-title').textContent = data.title;
463	          const dateEl = document.getElementById('caption-date');
464	          dateEl.textContent = data.date;
465	          dateEl.setAttribute('datetime', data.date);
466	          document.getElementById('hero-caption').classList.add('visible');
467	        }
468	      })
469	      .catch(() => {});
470	
471	    img.addEventListener('load', onImageReady);
472	
473	    // Copy buttons
474	    document.querySelectorAll('.copy-btn').forEach(btn => {
475	      btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
476	        const text = btn.dataset.copy;
477	        navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(() => {
478	          btn.classList.add('copied');
479	          setTimeout(() => btn.classList.remove('copied'), 2000);
480	        });
481	      });
482	    });
483	
484	    // Scroll fade-in
485	    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
486	      entries.forEach(entry => {
487	        if (entry.isIntersecting) {
488	          entry.target.classList.add('visible');
489	          observer.unobserve(entry.target);
490	        }
491	      });
492	    }, { threshold: 0.15 });
493	
494	    document.querySelectorAll('.fade-up').forEach(el => observer.observe(el));
495	  </script>
496	</body>
497	</html>
498
```
</details></tool-use>

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 09:51:46Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 09:51:49Z)**_

It's a waterfall problem. Right now the page does:

1. Load page → fetch `/info` from Worker → Worker fetches NASA API → returns JSON
2. *Then* set `img.src` to the NASA HD URL → browser fetches a multi-MB image directly from NASA

The image can't even start loading until step 1 finishes. Two fixes:

1. **Set the image src to the proxy endpoint directly** (`API_BASE + "/"`) in HTML instead of waiting for `/info`. The image starts loading immediately, and the Worker serves it cached.
2. **Fetch `/info` in parallel** just for the caption text.

This eliminates the waterfall — both requests fire at page load instead of sequentially. Want me to make that change?

+Next session starts with QA before deploying. Key changes to verify:
+
+1. **Workers Cache API** (`caches.default`) replaced in-memory Map + cf fetch options — cache should persist across wrangler reloads locally

+---
+name: Next session QA plan
+description: QA the Cache API and dev logging changes before deploying to production
+type: project

+
+Next session starts with QA before deploying. Key changes to verify:
+
+1. **Workers Cache API** (`caches.default`) replaced in-memory Map + cf fetch options — cache should persist across wrangler reloads locally
+2. **No date param** for "current" APOD requests — NASA handles "today" timezone logic
+3. **Per-request log buffer** — `log[]` array threaded through functions instead of module-level `logBuffer`
+4. **Dev logging format** — horizontal rule separator, then request summary, then indented cache lines, then wrangler's own line
+
+**Why:** Previous in-memory cache was wiped on every wrangler reload, burning through DEMO_KEY rate limits (30 req/hr). The UTC date bug caused 404s from NASA after midnight UTC but before midnight Eastern.
+
+**How to apply:** Test all routes (`/`, `/sd`, `/info`, `/about`) — verify cache HITs on second request, verify logs are clean, then deploy.
+

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 10:07:05Z)**_

A CNAME record:

```
www  →  nasapicture.com
```

Since you're on Cloudflare, add it in the DNS dashboard as a CNAME with name `www`, target `nasapicture.com`, proxied (orange cloud). Cloudflare will handle the redirect to the apex domain.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 10:10:12Z)**_

Here's what's good and what's missing:

**Already solid:**
- `lang="en"`, charset, viewport
- `<title>` and `<meta description>`
- OG title, description, image, type
- Twitter card
- Semantic HTML (`<main>`, `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<figure>`, `<footer>`)
- Single `<h1>`

**Missing / should add:**

1. **`og:url`** — needed for proper canonical sharing
2. **`twitter:title`** and **`twitter:description`** — some crawlers don't fall back to OG tags
3. **Canonical URL** — `<link rel="canonical">` prevents duplicate content issues (www vs non-www)
4. **Favicon** — browsers request it anyway; missing one causes 404s in logs
5. **`og:image` may not work** — the proxy returns `image/jpeg` but some crawlers expect a static image URL with a file extension. `/image.jpg` would be more reliable here.
6. **Structured data (JSON-LD)** — optional but would help with rich results (WebSite or WebApplication schema)
7. **`robots` meta** — not strictly needed (defaults to index/follow) but explicit is good practice
8. **`theme-color`** — for browser chrome on mobile

Want me to add these? The high-impact ones are canonical, og:url, twitter tags, and switching og:image to `/image.jpg`.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 10:16:20Z)**_

Love it. The Pale Blue Dot reference is perfect thematically — way more meaningful than a generic red accent. And the lens highlight sells "picture" without being literal. Two things I'd flag:

2. **Which blue?** — `nasa-blue` (#0032A0) might be too dark against the void background and would disappear in dark browser tabs. A lighter blue closer to the actual pale blue dot color (something like #6b9bd2 or #7eb4e2) would pop better as both the logo dot and favicon.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 10:38:10Z)**_

This hides the `.` text but keeps its natural space, then draws a circular dot via `::after` centered on that space. The `0.5em` size is relative to the logo font size — adjust if you want it bigger or smaller.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 12:38:00Z)**_

Nice changes. A few things I noticed:

1. **Bug**: `@media (min-width: 1024)` is missing `px` — that rule won't apply.

2. **`min-height: calc(50vh)`** — the `calc()` is unnecessary, can just be `min-height: 50vh` (minor).

3. **`.logo-dot::before` has `opacity: 0`** — the lens highlight is invisible. Intentional placeholder for later, or should it be visible?

4. **`margin-bottom: 3rem` on `.hero`** + `margin-bottom: 3rem` on `.hero-image-area` stacks to 6rem of space before the usage row. Might be more than intended.

Everything else looks solid — responsive font scaling, hiding nav on mobile, moving caption min-width behind the breakpoint, constraining `.info` width. Want me to fix the `1024` typo?

---

+```md
+---
+name: Next session QA plan
+description: QA the Cache API and dev logging changes before deploying to production
+type: project
+---
+
+Next session starts with QA before deploying. Key changes to verify:
+
+1. **Workers Cache API** (`caches.default`) replaced in-memory Map + cf fetch options — cache should persist across wrangler reloads locally
+2. **No date param** for "current" APOD requests — NASA handles "today" timezone logic
+3. **Per-request log buffer** — `log[]` array threaded through functions instead of module-level `logBuffer`
+4. **Dev logging format** — horizontal rule separator, then request summary, then indented cache lines, then wrangler's own line
+
+**Why:** Previous in-memory cache was wiped on every wrangler reload, burning through DEMO_KEY rate limits (30 req/hr). The UTC date bug caused 404s from NASA after midnight UTC but before midnight Eastern.
+
+**How to apply:** Test all routes (`/`, `/sd`, `/info`, `/about`) — verify cache HITs on second request, verify logs are clean, then deploy.
+
+```

Base directory for this skill: /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/skills/add

# Add Board Item

Create a new item in the `board/` project management system. The system spec is bundled with the plugin at `/Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/spec/board-system-spec.md`.

## Existing Board Items

Pre-loaded board data for duplicate detection:

filepath	type	status	priority	blocked_by	parent	title
./board/tasks/TASK-0002_deploy-landing-page.md	task	todo	-	-	NOTE-0002	Deploy landing page to Cloudflare Pages
./board/tasks/TASK-0003_nasa-data-insignia.md	task	todo	-	-	-	Apply for NASA Data Insignia
./board/tasks/TASK-0004_qa-cache-logging-deploy.md	task	todo	-	-	-	QA cache and logging before deploy
./board/planning/NOTE-0001_domain-strategy.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Domain strategy for nasapicture.com
./board/planning/NOTE-0002_landing-page.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Landing page for nasapicture.com
./board/planning/NOTE-0003_landing-page-critique.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Landing page design critique (gallery revision)

Before creating, check if a similar item already exists. If a close match is found, ask the user if they still want to create a new one.

## Scope Assessment

When the user requests a **TASK**, evaluate whether it is actually EPIC-sized before creating it. Look for these signals:

- Multiple distinct steps or deliverables in the description
- Multiple action verbs joined by "and" (e.g., "add auth and set up roles and integrate SSO")
- Work that spans different code areas or concerns
- Sub-steps that could be completed and shipped independently

**Quick test:** "Could someone meaningfully complete and ship part of this while the rest remains undone?" If yes → likely an EPIC.

If EPIC signals are present, suggest an EPIC + sub-TASKs with a concrete breakdown. For example:

> "This looks like it has multiple independent deliverables. Want me to create an EPIC with separate TASKs for each piece instead?"

**This is a soft suggestion only.** If the user says "just make it a task," comply without pushback.

This assessment only applies to TASK creation — do not second-guess EPICs, NOTEs, or other types.

## Arguments

The user provides the type and title: `/board:board-add task <title>`, `/board:board-add epic <title>`, etc.

If no type is given, ask the user what type of item to create.

## Determine Next ID

Run the ID assignment script:

```bash
bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/next-id.sh <TYPE> ./board
```

This scans all directories (including `board/archive/` and `.doc-ids`) and returns the next available 4-digit zero-padded ID.

## Create the File

Use the correct directory, frontmatter, and naming convention:

| Type | Directory | Filename Example |
|------|-----------|-----------------|
| task | `board/tasks/` | `TASK-0014_add-batch-processing.md` |
| epic | `board/planning/` | `EPIC-0009_batch-mode.md` |
| adr | `board/planning/` | `ADR-0002_chose-webgpu-over-wasm.md` |
| note | `board/planning/` | `NOTE-0001_explore-wasm-option.md` |
| reference | `board/reference/` | `REFE-0007_webgpu-overview.md` |
| resource | `board/resources/` | `RESO-0001_webgpu-article.md` |

## Frontmatter Templates

Task:
```yaml
---
type: task
status: backlog
priority: 2
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Epic:
```yaml
---
type: epic
status: planning
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

ADR:
```yaml
---
type: adr
status: active
context_date: YYYY-MM-DD
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Note:
```yaml
---
type: note
status: inbox
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Reference:
```yaml
---
type: reference
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Resource (ask user for URL):
```yaml
---
type: resource
status: inbox
url: <url>
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

## Naming Rules

- Convert title to kebab-case for filename
- Task titles MUST be imperative mood (start with a verb). If the user gives a noun phrase, rewrite it (e.g., "batch processing" -> "Add batch processing support")
- Use today's date for `created`

## After Creation

Show the file path and ask if the user wants to add more detail to the body.

_**User (2026-03-30 13:26:28Z)**_

is it weird to put the repo URL in the README. nearly everyone will view it on Github but I feel like when looking at the files directly thats useful

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 13:26:34Z)**_

Not weird at all. The README isn't only viewed on GitHub — it's also what someone sees when they clone the repo, browse it locally, or view it on a mirror. Having the repo URL right there is practical, not redundant.

---

_**User (2026-03-30 13:27:48Z)**_

whats the standard convention for the repo URL and personal attribution

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 13:27:56Z)**_

Typically the repo URL goes at the top (right after the title/description), and personal attribution goes at the bottom in a section or the footer. Common patterns:

```markdown
# Project Name

> Short description

[github.com/user/repo](https://github.com/user/repo)

...all the content...

## Author

[Grant Keller](https://grantkeller.dev)
```

Some people use a badge or just a one-liner at the very end. The key convention is: **repo/project info up top, people at the bottom.**

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-30 13:28:45Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./README.md`

```text
1	https://github.com/GDKeller/nasa-picture-proxy
2	
3	# NASA Picture Proxy
4	
5	A Cloudflare Worker that proxies [NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day](https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/astropix.html) behind a fixed URL. An HTTP GET request returns today's image without needing an API key or parsing JSON.
6	
7	During a tinkering session, I wanted to set the background image of my homelab [homepage](https://github.com/gethomepage/homepage) dashboard to the APOD. I didn't want to inject additional JavaScript on the page and couldn't find an existing static URL. So, I built this proxy to share with anyone else who'd like to view the wonders of space easily, anywhere.
8	
9	
10	## Usage
11	
12	Embed directly as an image:
13	
14	```html
15	<img src="https://api.nasapicture.com/" alt="NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day" />
16	```
17	
18	CSS background:
19	
20	```css
21	body {
22	  background-image: url("https://api.nasapicture.com/");
23	  background-size: cover;
24	}
25	```
26	
27	Fetch metadata:
28	
29	```bash
30	curl https://api.nasapicture.com/info | jq .
31	```
32	
33	## Endpoints
34	
35	| Path | Response |
36	|------|----------|
37	| `/` | Today's APOD image (HD, falls back to standard) |
38	| `/sd` | Standard resolution image |
39	| `/info` | JSON metadata (title, date, explanation, copyright) |
40	| `/about` | Plain-text description and route listing |
41	
42	When the APOD of the day is a video, the proxy walks backwards up to 7 days to find the most recent image.
43	
44	## How it works
45	
46	Both the NASA API call and the upstream image fetch are cached at Cloudflare's edge via `cf: { cacheTtl, cacheEverything }`. Caching is purely edge-based; there's no KV, D1, or cron involved.
47	
48	When the date rolls over, `formatDate(new Date())` produces a new date string, which changes the API request URL and therefore the cache key. The response updates automatically.
49	
50	## Author
51	
52	Built by [Grant Keller](https://grantkeller.dev).
53
```
</details></tool-use>

# Add Board Item

Create a new item in the `board/` project management system. The system spec is bundled with the plugin at `/Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/spec/board-system-spec.md`.

## Existing Board Items

Pre-loaded board data for duplicate detection:

filepath	type	status	priority	blocked_by	parent	title
./board/tasks/TASK-0002_deploy-landing-page.md	task	todo	-	-	NOTE-0002	Deploy landing page to Cloudflare Pages
./board/tasks/TASK-0003_nasa-data-insignia.md	task	todo	-	-	-	Apply for NASA Data Insignia
./board/tasks/TASK-0004_qa-cache-logging-deploy.md	task	todo	-	-	-	QA cache and logging before deploy
./board/planning/NOTE-0001_domain-strategy.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Domain strategy for nasapicture.com
./board/planning/NOTE-0002_landing-page.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Landing page for nasapicture.com
./board/planning/NOTE-0003_landing-page-critique.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Landing page design critique (gallery revision)
./board/planning/NOTE-0004_add-image-extension-routes-to-docs.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Add /image.jpg and /image-sd.jpg references to landing page and README

Before creating, check if a similar item already exists. If a close match is found, ask the user if they still want to create a new one.

## Scope Assessment

When the user requests a **TASK**, evaluate whether it is actually EPIC-sized before creating it. Look for these signals:

- Multiple distinct steps or deliverables in the description
- Multiple action verbs joined by "and" (e.g., "add auth and set up roles and integrate SSO")
- Work that spans different code areas or concerns
- Sub-steps that could be completed and shipped independently

**Quick test:** "Could someone meaningfully complete and ship part of this while the rest remains undone?" If yes → likely an EPIC.

If EPIC signals are present, suggest an EPIC + sub-TASKs with a concrete breakdown. For example:

> "This looks like it has multiple independent deliverables. Want me to create an EPIC with separate TASKs for each piece instead?"

**This is a soft suggestion only.** If the user says "just make it a task," comply without pushback.

This assessment only applies to TASK creation — do not second-guess EPICs, NOTEs, or other types.

## Arguments

The user provides the type and title: `/board:board-add task <title>`, `/board:board-add epic <title>`, etc.

If no type is given, ask the user what type of item to create.

## Determine Next ID

Run the ID assignment script:

```bash
bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/next-id.sh <TYPE> ./board
```

This scans all directories (including `board/archive/` and `.doc-ids`) and returns the next available 4-digit zero-padded ID.

## Create the File

Use the correct directory, frontmatter, and naming convention:

| Type | Directory | Filename Example |
|------|-----------|-----------------|
| task | `board/tasks/` | `TASK-0014_add-batch-processing.md` |
| epic | `board/planning/` | `EPIC-0009_batch-mode.md` |
| adr | `board/planning/` | `ADR-0002_chose-webgpu-over-wasm.md` |
| note | `board/planning/` | `NOTE-0001_explore-wasm-option.md` |
| reference | `board/reference/` | `REFE-0007_webgpu-overview.md` |
| resource | `board/resources/` | `RESO-0001_webgpu-article.md` |

## Frontmatter Templates

Task:
```yaml
---
type: task
status: backlog
priority: 2
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Epic:
```yaml
---
type: epic
status: planning
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

ADR:
```yaml
---
type: adr
status: active
context_date: YYYY-MM-DD
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Note:
```yaml
---
type: note
status: inbox
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Reference:
```yaml
---
type: reference
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Resource (ask user for URL):
```yaml
---
type: resource
status: inbox
url: <url>
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

## Naming Rules

- Convert title to kebab-case for filename
- Task titles MUST be imperative mood (start with a verb). If the user gives a noun phrase, rewrite it (e.g., "batch processing" -> "Add batch processing support")
- Use today's date for `created`

## After Creation

Show the file path and ask if the user wants to add more detail to the body.

+---
+name: Next session QA plan
+description: QA the Cache API and dev logging changes before deploying to production
+type: project
+---
+
+Next session starts with QA before deploying. Key changes to verify:
+
+1. **Workers Cache API** (`caches.default`) replaced in-memory Map + cf fetch options — cache should persist across wrangler reloads locally
+2. **No date param** for "current" APOD requests — NASA handles "today" timezone logic
+3. **Per-request log buffer** — `log[]` array threaded through functions instead of module-level `logBuffer`
+4. **Dev logging format** — horizontal rule separator, then request summary, then indented cache lines, then wrangler's own line
+
+**Why:** Previous in-memory cache was wiped on every wrangler reload, burning through DEMO_KEY rate limits (30 req/hr). The UTC date bug caused 404s from NASA after midnight UTC but before midnight Eastern.
+
+**How to apply:** Test all routes (`/`, `/sd`, `/info`, `/about`) — verify cache HITs on second request, verify logs are clean, then deploy.
+
+```

 # NASA Picture Proxy
 
-A Cloudflare Worker that proxies [NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day](https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/astropix.html) behind a fixed URL. An HTTP GET request returns today's image without needing an API key or parsing JSON.
+A Cloudflare Worker that proxies [NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day (APOD)](https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/astropix.html) behind a fixed URL. An HTTP GET request returns today's image without needing an API key or parsing JSON.
+
+[nasapicture.com](https://nasapicture.com) · [GitHub](https://github.com/GDKeller/nasa-picture-proxy)
+
+![Today's APOD](https://api.nasapicture.com/image.jpg)
 
 During a tinkering session, I wanted to set the background image of my homelab [homepage](https://github.com/gethomepage/homepage) dashboard to the APOD. I didn't want to inject additional JavaScript on the page and couldn't find an existing static URL. So, I built this proxy to share with anyone else who'd like to view the wonders of space easily, anywhere.
 
@@ -24,18 +26,36 @@ body {
 }
 ```
 
+**Need a file extension?** Some contexts (RSS feeds, Open Graph tags, markdown renderers) require a URL that ends in an image extension. You can add `/image.jpg` or `/image-sd.jpg` as well:  
+```
+https://api.nasapicture.com/image.jpg
+https://api.nasapicture.com/image-sd.jpg
+```  
+
 Fetch metadata:
 
 ```bash
 curl https://api.nasapicture.com/info | jq .
 ```
 
+Returns data from the APOD API:
+- Direct URLs of APOD images
+- Date
+- Image title
+- Image explanation
+- Copyright info
+
+> [!TIP]
+> For those without the tech savvy to understand (i.e. remember) "api", all routes are also accessible at `get.nasapicture.com`
+
 ## Endpoints
 
 | Path | Response |
 |------|----------|
 | `/` | Today's APOD image (HD, falls back to standard) |
+| `/image.jpg` | Same as `/` with file extension |
 | `/sd` | Standard resolution image |
+| `/image-sd.jpg` | Same as `/sd` with file extension |
 | `/info` | JSON metadata (title, date, explanation, copyright) |
 | `/about` | Plain-text description and route listing |
